lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਤੂ ਆਵਹਿ ਚਿਤਿ ਤਿਸ ਨੋ ਸਦਾ ਸੁਖ ॥
lines.translit: jis too aaveh chit tis no sadaa sukh |
lines.english: One who is conscious of You finds everlasting peace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨੁ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਮੰਗੈ ਦਾਨੁ ਇਕੁ ਦੇਹੁ ਦਰਸੁ ਮਨਿ ਪਿਆਰੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jan naanak mangai daan ik dehu daras man piaar |2|
lines.english: Servant Nanak begs for this one gift: please bless me, Lord, with the Blessed Vision of Your Darshan; my mind is in love with You. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਤੂ ਆਵਹਿ ਚਿਤਿ ਤਿਸੁ ਕਿ ਕਾੜਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: jis too aaveh chit tis ki kaarriaa |
lines.english: One who is conscious of You is not anxious.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਤੂ ਆਵਹਿ ਚਿਤਿ ਤਿਸੁ ਜਮ ਨਾਹਿ ਦੁਖ ॥
lines.translit: jis too aaveh chit tis jam naeh dukh |
lines.english: One who is conscious of You does not suffer at the hands of the Messenger of Death.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਤੂ ਆਵਹਿ ਚਿਤਿ ਬਹੁਤਾ ਤਿਸੁ ਧਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: jis too aaveh chit bahutaa tis dhan |
lines.english: One who is conscious of You becomes very wealthy.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਦਾ ਕਰਤਾ ਮਿਤ੍ਰੁ ਸਭਿ ਕਾਜ ਸਵਾਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: jis daa karataa mitru sabh kaaj savaariaa |
lines.english: One who has the Creator as his Friend - all his affairs are resolved.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਤੂ ਆਵਹਿ ਚਿਤਿ ਸੋ ਪਰਵਾਣੁ ਜਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: jis too aaveh chit so paravaan jan |
lines.english: One who is conscious of You is renowned and respected.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਤੂ ਆਵਹਿ ਚਿਤਿ ਸੋ ਵਡ ਪਰਵਾਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: jis too aaveh chit so vadd paravaariaa |
lines.english: One who is conscious of You has a great family.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਤੂ ਆਵਹਿ ਚਿਤਿ ਤਿਨਿ ਕੁਲ ਉਧਾਰਿਆ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: jis too aaveh chit tin kul udhaariaa |6|
lines.english: One who is conscious of You saves his ancestors. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੫ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 5 |
lines.english: Salok, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਹੀ ਧੋਵੈ ਚਕ੍ਰ ਬਣਾਏ ਮਾਇਆ ਨੋ ਬਹੁ ਧਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: dehee dhovai chakr banaae maaeaa no bahu dhaavai |
lines.english: He washes his body, and draws ritual marks on it, and totally runs after wealth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਦਰਹੁ ਅੰਨਾ ਬਾਹਰਹੁ ਅੰਨਾ ਕੂੜੀ ਕੂੜੀ ਗਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: andarahu anaa baaharahu anaa koorree koorree gaavai |
lines.english: Blind inwardly, and blind outwardly, he sings falsely, falsely.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਦਰਿ ਮੈਲੁ ਨ ਉਤਰੈ ਹਉਮੈ ਫਿਰਿ ਫਿਰਿ ਆਵੈ ਜਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: andar mail na utarai haumai fir fir aavai jaavai |
lines.english: But the filth of his egotism is not removed from within, and over and over again, he comes and goes in reincarnation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨੀਂਦ ਵਿਆਪਿਆ ਕਾਮਿ ਸੰਤਾਪਿਆ ਮੁਖਹੁ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਕਹਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: neend viaapiaa kaam santaapiaa mukhahu har har kahaavai |
lines.english: Engulfed in sleep, and tormented by frustrated sexual desire, he chants the Lord's Name with his mouth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੰਸਾ ਵਿਚਿ ਬੈਠਾ ਬਗੁ ਨ ਬਣਈ ਨਿਤ ਬੈਠਾ ਮਛੀ ਨੋ ਤਾਰ ਲਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: hansaa vich baitthaa bag na banee nit baitthaa machhee no taar laavai |
lines.english: Sitting among the swans, the crane does not become one of them; sitting there, he keeps staring at the fish.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬੈਸਨੋ ਨਾਮੁ ਕਰਮ ਹਉ ਜੁਗਤਾ ਤੁਹ ਕੁਟੇ ਕਿਆ ਫਲੁ ਪਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: baisano naam karam hau jugataa tuh kutte kiaa fal paavai |
lines.english: He is called a Vaishnav, but he is bound to deeds of egotism; by threshing only husks, what rewards can be obtained?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਹੰਸ ਸਭਾ ਵੀਚਾਰੁ ਕਰਿ ਦੇਖਨਿ ਤਾ ਬਗਾ ਨਾਲਿ ਜੋੜੁ ਕਦੇ ਨ ਆਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: jaa hans sabhaa veechaar kar dekhan taa bagaa naal jorr kade na aavai |
lines.english: And when the gathering of swans looks and sees, they realize that they can never form an alliance with the crane.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੰਸਾ ਹੀਰਾ ਮੋਤੀ ਚੁਗਣਾ ਬਗੁ ਡਡਾ ਭਾਲਣ ਜਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: hansaa heeraa motee chuganaa bag ddaddaa bhaalan jaavai |
lines.english: The swans peck at the diamonds and pearls, while the crane chases after frogs.

lines.gurmukhi: ਉਡਰਿਆ ਵੇਚਾਰਾ ਬਗੁਲਾ ਮਤੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਮੰਞੁ ਲਖਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: auddariaa vechaaraa bagulaa mat hovai many lakhaavai |
lines.english: The poor crane flies away, so that his secret will not be exposed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਤੁ ਕੋ ਲਾਇਆ ਤਿਤ ਹੀ ਲਾਗਾ ਕਿਸੁ ਦੋਸੁ ਦਿਚੈ ਜਾ ਹਰਿ ਏਵੈ ਭਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: jit ko laaeaa tith hee laagaa kis dos dichai jaa har evai bhaavai |
lines.english: Whatever the Lord attaches one to, to that he is attached. Who is to blame, when the Lord wills it so?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸਰਵਰੁ ਰਤਨੀ ਭਰਪੂਰੇ ਜਿਸੁ ਪ੍ਰਾਪਤਿ ਸੋ ਪਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: satigur saravar ratanee bharapoore jis praapat so paavai |
lines.english: The True Guru is the lake, overflowing with pearls. One who meets the True Guru obtains them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਖ ਹੰਸ ਸਰਵਰਿ ਇਕਠੇ ਹੋਏ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਹੁਕਮਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: sikh hans saravar ikatthe hoe satigur kai hukamaavai |
lines.english: The Sikh-swans gather at the lake, according to the Will of the True Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਤਨ ਪਦਾਰਥ ਮਾਣਕ ਸਰਵਰਿ ਭਰਪੂਰੇ ਖਾਇ ਖਰਚਿ ਰਹੇ ਤੋਟਿ ਨ ਆਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: ratan padaarath maanak saravar bharapoore khaae kharach rahe tott na aavai |
lines.english: The lake is filled with the wealth of these jewels and pearls; they are spent and consumed, but they never run out.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਰਵਰ ਹੰਸੁ ਦੂਰਿ ਨ ਹੋਈ ਕਰਤੇ ਏਵੈ ਭਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: saravar hans door na hoee karate evai bhaavai |
lines.english: The swan never leaves the lake; such is the Pleasure of the Creator's Will.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਜਿਸ ਦੈ ਮਸਤਕਿ ਭਾਗੁ ਧੁਰਿ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਸੋ ਸਿਖੁ ਗੁਰੂ ਪਹਿ ਆਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: jan naanak jis dai masatak bhaag dhur likhiaa so sikh guroo peh aavai |
lines.english: O servant Nanak, one who has such pre-ordained destiny inscribed upon his forehead - that Sikh comes to the Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਤਰਿਆ ਕੁਟੰਬ ਸਭਿ ਤਾਰੇ ਸਭਾ ਸ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਛਡਾਵੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: aap tariaa kuttanb sabh taare sabhaa srisatt chhaddaavai |1|
lines.english: He saves himself, and saves all his generations as well; he emancipates the whole world. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਡਿਤੁ ਆਖਾਏ ਬਹੁਤੀ ਰਾਹੀ ਕੋਰੜ ਮੋਠ ਜਿਨੇਹਾ ॥
lines.translit: panddit aakhaae bahutee raahee korarr motth jinehaa |
lines.english: He is called a Pandit, a religious scholar, and yet he wanders along many pathways. He is as hard as uncooked beans.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਦਰਿ ਮੋਹੁ ਨਿਤ ਭਰਮਿ ਵਿਆਪਿਆ ਤਿਸਟਸਿ ਨਾਹੀ ਦੇਹਾ ॥
lines.translit: andar mohu nit bharam viaapiaa tisattas naahee dehaa |
lines.english: He is filled with attachment, and constantly engrossed in doubt; his body cannot hold still.

lines.translit: mahalaa 5 |
lines.english: Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੂੜੀ ਆਵੈ ਕੂੜੀ ਜਾਵੈ ਮਾਇਆ ਕੀ ਨਿਤ ਜੋਹਾ ॥
lines.translit: koorree aavai koorree jaavai maaeaa kee nit johaa |
lines.english: False is his coming, and false is his going; he is continually on the lookout for Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੁ ਕਹੈ ਤਾ ਛੋਹੋ ਆਵੈ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਬਹੁਤਾ ਰੋਹਾ ॥
lines.translit: sach kahai taa chhoho aavai antar bahutaa rohaa |
lines.english: If someone speaks the truth, then he is aggravated; he is totally filled with anger.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਆਪਿਆ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਕੁਬੁਧਿ ਕੁਮੂੜਾ ਮਨਿ ਲਾਗਾ ਤਿਸੁ ਮੋਹਾ ॥
lines.translit: viaapiaa duramat kubudh kumoorraa man laagaa tis mohaa |
lines.english: The evil fool is engrossed in evil-mindedness and false intellectualizations; his mind is attached to emotional attachment.

lines.gurmukhi: ਠਗੈ ਸੇਤੀ ਠਗੁ ਰਲਿ ਆਇਆ ਸਾਥੁ ਭਿ ਇਕੋ ਜੇਹਾ ॥
lines.translit: tthagai setee tthag ral aaeaa saath bhi iko jehaa |
lines.english: The deceiver abides with the five deceivers; it is a gathering of like minds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸਰਾਫੁ ਨਦਰੀ ਵਿਚਦੋ ਕਢੈ ਤਾਂ ਉਘੜਿ ਆਇਆ ਲੋਹਾ ॥
lines.translit: satigur saraaf nadaree vichado kadtai taan ugharr aaeaa lohaa |
lines.english: And when the Jeweller, the True Guru, appraises him, then he is exposed as mere iron.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਹੁਤੇਰੀ ਥਾਈ ਰਲਾਇ ਰਲਾਇ ਦਿਤਾ ਉਘੜਿਆ ਪੜਦਾ ਅਗੈ ਆਇ ਖਲੋਹਾ ॥
lines.translit: bahuteree thaaee ralaae ralaae ditaa ugharriaa parradaa agai aae khalohaa |
lines.english: Mixed and mingled with others, he was passed off as genuine in many places; but now, the veil has been lifted, and he stands naked before all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਜੇ ਸਰਣੀ ਆਵੈ ਫਿਰਿ ਮਨੂਰਹੁ ਕੰਚਨੁ ਹੋਹਾ ॥
lines.translit: satigur kee je saranee aavai fir manoorahu kanchan hohaa |
lines.english: One who comes to the Sanctuary of the True Guru, shall be transformed from iron into gold.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਜਿਸੁ ਧੁਰਿ ਮਸਤਕਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਤਿਸੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਨਾਲਿ ਸਨੇਹਾ ॥
lines.translit: naanak jis dhur masatak hovai likhiaa tis satigur naal sanehaa |
lines.english: O Nanak, one who has such pre-ordained destiny inscribed upon his forehead, is in love with the True Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਨਿਰਵੈਰੁ ਪੁਤ੍ਰ ਸਤ੍ਰ ਸਮਾਨੇ ਅਉਗਣ ਕਟੇ ਕਰੇ ਸੁਧੁ ਦੇਹਾ ॥
lines.translit: satigur niravair putr satr samaane aaugan katte kare sudh dehaa |
lines.english: The True Guru has no anger or vengeance; He looks upon son and enemy alike. Removing faults and mistakes, He purifies the human body.
